title: cephalotaxus drupacea var. koreana
intro: Cephalotaxus drupacea var. koreana, also known as Korean plum yew, is a variety of the evergreen coniferous tree Cephalotaxus drupacea native to the mountainous regions of Korea. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and antitumor activities. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving cough and sore throat, and protecting against certain types of cancer. In traditional medicine, Cephalotaxus drupacea var. koreana is often prepared as an infusion, tincture, or decoction, and is used to treat a range of ailments, including respiratory infections and digestive issues.
